brief_summary: Two-column comparison diagram showing the scope of Katakana legend coverage in R1 (2020) versus R2 (2026). R1 column shows alphas in coral (Katakana) and nav cluster + numpad in muted grey (Latin only). R2 column shows all three blocks in coral with labels indicating Katakana nav cluster and Precision Kit numpad. A bracketed callout beneath the R2 column reads 'full-layout thematic coverage.' Illustrates the core design upgrade claim made in the article.
data_sources: gmk-cyl-masterpiece-r2-group-buy.mdx § 'The design argument' — 'Katakana legends were confined to the alpha keys — a choice that reads as caution in retrospect. The navigation cluster kept Latin glyphs. The numpad kept Latin glyphs.'

gmk-cyl-masterpiece-r2-group-buy.mdx § 'The design argument' — 'R2 fixes this at the technical level, not just the editorial one. The navigation cluster is 1u keys — Home, End, PgUp, PgDn, Insert, Delete, and the arrow cluster. Energieschleuder extended Katakana across the full nav cluster and into the numpad.'
gmk-cyl-masterpiece-r2-group-buy.mdx § 'Kitting and pricing' — 'The Precision Kit at $25' and 'R1 ran a single base kit at $129 USD with numpad included.'
